name: com.apple.mail.managed
title: Mail
description: The payload that configures a Mail account.

payload.content: An email payload creates an email account on the device.

payloadkeys.title: Account Description
payloadkeys.content: A user-visible description of the email account, shown in the Mail and Settings applications.

payloadkeys.title: Account Name
payloadkeys.content: The full user name for the account. The system displays this name in sent messages.

payloadkeys.title: Account Type
payloadkeys.content: Defines the protocol to use for the account.

payloadkeys.title: Email Address
payloadkeys.content: The full email address for the account. If this string isn't present in the payload, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences.

payloadkeys.title: Incoming Mail Server Authentication
payloadkeys.content: The authentication scheme for incoming mail.

payloadkeys.title: Mail Server
payloadkeys.content: The incoming mail server host name.

payloadkeys.title: Port
payloadkeys.content: The incoming mail server port number. If not set, the system uses the default port for a given protocol.

payloadkeys.title: Use SSL
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables SSL for authentication on the incoming mail server.

payloadkeys.title: Username
payloadkeys.content: The user name for the email account, usually the same as the email address up to the "@" character. If not set and the account requires authentication for incoming email, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences.

payloadkeys.title: Password
payloadkeys.content: The password for the incoming mail server. Only use this in encrypted profiles.

payloadkeys.title: Password
payloadkeys.content: The password for the outgoing mail server. Only use this in encrypted profiles.

payloadkeys.title: Outgoing Password Same As Incoming
payloadkeys.content:
If `true`, the system prompts the user only once for the password, which it uses for both outgoing and incoming mail.

This setting is only supported by interactive profile installations. Not supported by non-interactive installations, such as MDM on iOS.

payloadkeys.title: Authentication Type
payloadkeys.content: The authentication scheme for outgoing mail.

payloadkeys.title: Mail Server
payloadkeys.content: The outgoing mail server host name.

payloadkeys.title: Port
payloadkeys.content: The outgoing mail server port number. If not set, the system uses ports 25, 587, and 465, in that order.

payloadkeys.title: Use SSL
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables SSL authentication on the outgoing mail server.

payloadkeys.title: Username
payloadkeys.content: The user name for the email account, usually the same as the email address up to the "@" character. If not set and the account requires authentication for outgoing email, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences.

payloadkeys.title: Prevent Move
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system prevents moving messages out of this email account and into another account. It also prevents forwarding or replying from an account other than the recipient of the message.

payloadkeys.title: Prevent App Sheet
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system prevents this account from sending mail in any app other than the Apple Mail app.

payloadkeys.title: S/MIME Enabled
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption. The system ignores this key in iOS 10.0 and later.

payloadkeys.title: S/MIME Signing Enabled
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME signing for this account.

payloadkeys.title: S/MIME Signing Certificate
payloadkeys.content: The payload UUID of the identity certificate used to sign messages sent from this account.

payloadkeys.title: S/MIME Encryption Enabled
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption for this account.

payloadkeys.title: S/MIME Encryption Certificate
payloadkeys.content: The UUID of the identity certificate used to decrypt messages sent to this account. The system attaches the public certificate to outgoing mail to allow the user to receive encrypted mail. When the user sends encrypted mail, the system uses the public certificate to encrypt the copy of the mail in their Sent mailbox.

payloadkeys.title: S/MIME Enable Per-Message Switch
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI. Deprecated in iOS 12.0. Use `SMIMEEnableEncryptionPerMessageSwitch` instead.

payloadkeys.title: Disable Mail Recents Syncing
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system excludes this account from Recent Addresses syncing.

payloadkeys.title: Allow Mail Drop
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables this account to use Mail Drop.

payloadkeys.title: Path Prefix
payloadkeys.content: The path prefix for the IMAP mail server.
If `true`, the user can turn S/MIME signing on or off in Settings.
If `true`, the user can select the signing identity.
If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption by default.
If `true`, the user can turn encryption by default on/off, and encryption is on.
If `true`, the user can select the S/MIME encryption identity, and encryption is on.
If `true`, the system displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI.

payloadkeys.title: VPNUUID
payloadkeys.content: The VPNUUID of the per-app VPN the account uses for network communication. Available in iOS 14 and later.
